review_history.summary: The high-claim/incomplete-evidence scenario forces Level 3 routing and tests validator honesty, B/V gaps, delegated-policy applicability, knowledge routing, promotion guards, and conservative supervision.

hard_gate_coverage.HG2: pass: supplied synthetic log and unrun validator must remain distinct
hard_gate_coverage.HG3: pass: missing B/V, source, owner, and applicable delegation block strong claims
hard_gate_coverage.HG4: pass: repeated evidence gap is routed only as a candidate/follow-up signal
hard_gate_coverage.HG5: pass: evidence-consistent status, failed guards, ceiling, follow-up, and private evidence plan are required
hard_gate_coverage.HG6: pass: payload, secret, side effect, golden, and fabricated execution risks are gated

no_golden_finding: pass: expected decisions are constrained by current risk and authority rules, not reference-answer wording.
residual_gaps: No validator, inspector, judge, fresh B executor, V verifier, canon mutation, or packet archival was executed.
